Stub functions for initializing, starting, feeding, and checking the reset flag of a watchdog timer.

# Purpose
This code is a C source file that provides basic functions for managing a watchdog timer in an embedded system. It includes the header file `drivers/watchdog.h` and defines several functions: [`watchdog_init`](<#watchdog_init>), [`watchdog_start`](<#watchdog_start>), [`watchdog_feed`](<#watchdog_feed>), [`watchdog_check_reset_flag`](<#watchdog_check_reset_flag>), and [`watchdog_clear_reset_flag`](<#watchdog_clear_reset_flag>). The [`watchdog_init`](<#watchdog_init>), [`watchdog_start`](<#watchdog_start>), and [`watchdog_feed`](<#watchdog_feed>) functions are placeholders for initializing, starting, and feeding the watchdog timer, respectively. The [`watchdog_check_reset_flag`](<#watchdog_check_reset_flag>) function returns a boolean value indicating whether a reset flag is set, while the [`watchdog_clear_reset_flag`](<#watchdog_clear_reset_flag>) function returns a `McuRebootReason` structure with fields indicating the cause of the last microcontroller reset, with the `power_on_reset` field set to 1, indicating a power-on reset.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `drivers/watchdog.h`
